message:
fix(db): verify release_reservations via post-update count + pool quarantine error propagation

Queries (release_reservations): replaces the pre/post row-count
comparison with a post-UPDATE verification query that checks
remaining_active == 0. The old approach compared affected-row count
against the input slice length, which could give false negatives when
SQLite coalesced no-op updates (row already had the target status).
The new approach definitively confirms no active reservations remain
in the filtered set.

Pool (ensure_sqlite_file_healthy_with_archive): the quarantine rename
of a corrupted database now propagates errors instead of silently
ignoring them with let _ =. If the rename fails (e.g., read-only
filesystem), callers now receive a descriptive SqlError::Custom
rather than proceeding with stale file handles.

Additional refinements:
- pattern_overlap.rs: minor overlap detection logic adjustment
- coalesce.rs: coalescing edge case improvement
- guard/lib.rs: guard check refinements
- search-core/two_tier.rs: two-tier search scoring adjustments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
